subject: UPDATE FROM THE ROAD: Tennesseans can't wait
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
| | Dear Peter, We're halfway through the **Stand for Rural Healthcare Tour** and we want to share some of what we've heard. But first, here's why this tour is so important and what you can do to help. In America, you are free to choose where you live. And whether your choice is a big city, a suburb, a small town, or a rural community, and whether you're white, black or brown, tenth generation or newcomer, you should be free to thrive and reach your full potential wherever you live. Tennessee Democrats believe the rural way of life is worth fighting for. We refuse to sit back and watch as Tennesseans who choose to live in rural communities continue to lose ground in the economy and in life expectancy because threatening issues go unaddressed by the Republican majority in Nashville and Washington. Among the greatest threats to the rural way of life are hospital closures, being uninsured or underinsured, and opioid addiction, all left unaddressed and allowed to fester by TNGOP and their refusal to expand Medicaid in Tennessee. The bottom line is, no family can thrive and no community can prosper without access to physical and mental healthcare, emergency medical services, and hospitals. Unfortunately, the Republican answer to the healthcare and addiction crisis in Tennessee is 1) [create a "task force" to study it some more](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/2TIPru4WEuh5H_wXravEGSZxzacuIB0-cuewJjC_gDA=), 2) funnel $3 million dollars to [Navigant, a Chicago-based consulting firm](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/5aKlub1xLXeYjUjaN5m8m77q6g9GYeTV6CCyBMXbsV8=), and [other out-of-state consulting firms ](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/vfrXaW6VgT3parXifdHs5ozIVlYByHc1jZk2Wy4PYio=)to study it some more, and 3) with no plan on how to spend the money, [go through a lengthy application process to the federal government for an untested block grant program](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/MfftmPAWZc-yZXOxuEbWVsXfswRu_Qfr5xqi2PYudY4=), i.e., money from the federal government with no oversight mechanism and that experts say ["could force major cutbacks in health coverage for low-income people."](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/MfftmPAWZc-yZXOxuEbWVsXfswRu_Qfr5xqi2PYudY4=) Tennesseans can't wait. The [August 23 special legislative session](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/WOszu4By9RZXiyIfIsQzx8Eg_GADoQ12lMNNw7JP-H4=) called to elect a new speaker of the house must include a vote on the expansion of Medicaid to stop the epidemic of hospital and emergency room closures and to stop uninsured, underinsured, or opioid addicted Tennesseans from dying. Call or email (presumptive) Speaker of the House, Rep. Cameron Sexton) R-Crossville, at his office at (615) 741-2343 and tell him to expand Medicaid now! Here's some of what we've heard so far on the TNDP Stand for Rural Healthcare tour: **Clay County:** _" We have Dale Hollow Lake, one of the most beautiful lakes in Tennessee, but retirees are not coming to Celina anymore and the ones who are here are thinking of moving away. They say, 'I wish I didn't come' and 'I can't afford to be this far away from a hospital.'"_ **Fentress County:** _" How did the state allow a company that is 51 millions dollars in debt to buy another hospital?"_ _" Governor Lee, you talk about Tennessee tourism but without a hospital and a potential horse accident looming over their heads, people aren't going to want to come here."_ **Polk County:** _" In 1954, men who worked for 'the company' [_[https://www.tennessean.com/story/money/industries/health-care/2019/02/14/rural-hospital-closures-ducktown-tn-gov-bill-lee-copper-basin-medical-center/2118872002/](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/AncDDDgVqMe5ydI-2XwhdlzhUe_1dJcxKNuHvUdACtU=)] _brought the entire community together to build a hospital because they saw their co-workers killed and maimed in the mines and they saw the gravestones of all the children who died and the women who died in childbirth. These were Americans and proud union members who volunteered to contribute to the common cause and common community and were proud to do so. You know what this would be called today? Socialism. "_ _" It's bad enough having a sick or injured child let alone worrying about how to pay for it."_ _" It's not enough to make a living, people want to make a killing."_ _" If [State Senator] Mike Bell voted to expand Medicaid, the tax dollars we already paid would be coming home to keep our people healthy instead of going to Kentucky and Arkansas."_ **McMinn County:** _" What bothers me the most is the lip service paid to veterans while they get substandard healthcare."_ _" Three Tennessee counties are in the top 100 in the United States for opioid addictions. 1.3 million people have gone through treatment on expanded Medicaid."_ _" Most of the states that have expanded Medicaid have Republican governors."_ _" There are more fatalities now because people addicted to prescription opioids can't get them and are switching to street drugs like fentanyl and heroin."_ _" The block grant program proposed by Governor Lee has nothing to do with Medicaid expansion. With block grants, we would get 8 billion from the federal government with no strings attached. Meaning, we can spend it the way we want. But if we don't follow federal guidelines and costs rise, the fed does not pay for it. It's a medicine extraction not expansion."_ **Knox County:** _" Dealers are cutting fentanyl with ground glass..you know, as a filler so they have more to sell."_ To find out more about the tour or to get more involved as a storyteller, speaker, volunteer, or organizer during one of the stops, let us know by filling out this form or email Trevon Sylvester at [trevon@tndp.org](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/AyQJngiSBUNRykjdHjEXuYAxsIRvr_vWZY0j19pVbLA=?subject=Stand%20for%20Rural%20Healthcare%20Tour). More info at tndp.org/standforruralhealthcare [tndp.org/standforruralhealthcare] and check our [Facebook events page](https://act.myngp.com/el/Xnykl1s2JxIM4uewHEMNWAYudNnPKCz8APr-P7No-lg=/7D82g8mHBDdaVmMWyIw_xZstMdsETVOWcU8odjINXrc=) for details on all the tour stops. For social media, use #StandForRuralTN and #WeAreTN (thank you, Carol Abney!) #WeAreTN, Mary

Mary \--
Mary Mancini

Unfortunately, the Republican answer to the healthcare and addiction crisis in Tennessee is 1) =, 2) funnel $3 million dollars to =, and study it some more, and 3) with no plan on how to spend the money, =, i.e., money from the federal government with no oversight mechanism and that experts say = Tennesseans can't wait. The = called to elect a new speaker of the house must include a vote on the expansion of Medicaid to stop the epidemic of hospital and emergency room closures and to stop uninsured, underinsured, or opioid addicted Tennesseans from dying. Call or email (presumptive) Speaker of the House, Rep. Cameron Sexton) R-Crossville, at his office at (615) 741-2343 and tell him to expand Medicaid now! Here's some of what we've heard so far on the TNDP Stand for Rural Healthcare tour: Clay County: " We have Dale Hollow Lake, one of the most beautiful lakes in Tennessee, but retirees are not coming to Celina anymore and the ones who are here are thinking of moving away. They say, 'I wish I didn't come' and 'I can't afford to be this far away from a hospital.'" Fentress County: " How did the state allow a company that is 51 millions dollars in debt to buy another hospital?" " Governor Lee, you talk about Tennessee tourism but without a hospital and a potential horse accident looming over their heads, people aren't going to want to come here." Polk County: " In 1954, men who worked for 'the company' =] brought the entire community together to build a hospital because they saw their co-workers killed and maimed in the mines and they saw the gravestones of all the children who died and the women who died in childbirth. These were Americans and proud union members who volunteered to contribute to the common cause and common community and were proud to do so. You know what this would be called today? Socialism. " " It's bad enough having a sick or injured child let alone worrying about how to pay for it." " It's not enough to make a living, people want to make a killing." " If [State Senator] Mike Bell voted to expand Medicaid, the tax dollars we already paid would be coming home to keep our people healthy instead of going to Kentucky and Arkansas." McMinn County: " What bothers me the most is the lip service paid to veterans while they get substandard healthcare." " Three Tennessee counties are in the top 100 in the United States for opioid addictions. 1.3 million people have gone through treatment on expanded Medicaid." " Most of the states that have expanded Medicaid have Republican governors." " There are more fatalities now because people addicted to prescription opioids can't get them and are switching to street drugs like fentanyl and heroin." " The block grant program proposed by Governor Lee has nothing to do with Medicaid expansion. With block grants, we would get 8 billion from the federal government with no strings attached. Meaning, we can spend it the way we want. But if we don't follow federal guidelines and costs rise, the fed does not pay for it. It's a medicine extraction not expansion." Knox County: " Dealers are cutting fentanyl with ground glass..you know, as a filler so they have more to sell." To find out more about the tour or to get more involved as a storyteller, speaker, volunteer, or organizer during one of the stops, let us know by filling out this form or email Trevon Sylvester at . More info at tndp.org/standforruralhealthcare [tndp.org/standforruralhealthcare] and check our = for details on all the tour stops. name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Food insecurity
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter,

On Friday, I organized a group to volunteer at Feed More, our regional food bank to help our neighbors facing food insecurity.

Areas of Virginia's seventh district have food insecurity rates of over 20%. Additionally, more than 20,000 children face food insecurity in our district every single day. Hunger impacts everything from a child's ability to learn to a family's ability to feel secure to a senior's ability to manage her chronic illness. Last month, the administration announced a proposed change to the SNAP program that could threaten the eligibility of up to 3,000,000 SNAP recipients. [_**Sign our petition and stand in support of the federal programs that help so many families. Support our federal programs to fight hunger >>>**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/MrsTz3JtF_vTtRFKdk-4cWPpTxTJDtNYvqdy2djGVTE=/5K2h_srtedOSnYrJ0Ki2AIAQizhdAWLsH0NXAbls2Ko=)
| | | [ SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.myngp.com/el/MrsTz3JtF_vTtRFKdk-4cWPpTxTJDtNYvqdy2djGVTE=/5K2h_srtedOSnYrJ0Ki2AIAQizhdAWLsH0NXAbls2Ko=)
---
Addressing food insecurity in the long term requires a comprehensive approach and the recognition of the impact of Congress' economic, healthcare, and education policies on food insecurity and hunger.

Community programs, corporate and individual donors, and organizations like Feed More and its networks of food banks serve so many in our community. However, Congress must act to protect the programs and funding that help deliver services and relief to hungry families. I'm proud to serve on the House Agriculture Committee which covers programs that promote food security.

[_**Will you sign my petition and stand with me in support of the programs that help so many families facing hunger? >>>**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/MrsTz3JtF_vTtRFKdk-4cWPpTxTJDtNYvqdy2djGVTE=/5K2h_srtedOSnYrJ0Ki2AIAQizhdAWLsH0NXAbls2Ko=)

clean_body: Peter, On Friday, I organized a group to volunteer at Feed More, our regional food bank to help our neighbors facing food insecurity. Areas of Virginia's seventh district have food insecurity rates of over 20%. Additionally, more than 20,000 children face food insecurity in our district every single day. Hunger impacts everything from a child's ability to learn to a family's ability to feel secure to a senior's ability to manage her chronic illness. Last month, the administration announced a proposed change to the SNAP program that could threaten the eligibility of up to 3,000,000 SNAP recipients. = | | | = Addressing food insecurity in the long term requires a comprehensive approach and the recognition of the impact of Congress' economic, healthcare, and education policies on food insecurity and hunger. Community programs, corporate and individual donors, and organizations like Feed More and its networks of food banks serve so many in our community. However, Congress must act to protect the programs and funding that help deliver services and relief to hungry families. I'm proud to serve on the House Agriculture Committee which covers programs that promote food security. = | | = Thank you, = -Abigail * Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. When I stand there, surrounded by hopeful words, I take a moment to reflect on what's possible when we're all in this fight together.

**And next time I 'm in HQ, I'll get to visit our new Grassroots Donor Wall. I can't wait to see the names of the people powering this movement -- the people who are right by my side in this fight.**

>
We couldn't do any of this without the generous support of grassroots donors.

And you know what one of the best parts of running for president is? Getting to talk on the phone with people who've pitched in anything they can.

Every time I make one of those thank you calls, I'm humbled by the conversation we have. Each and every person who chips in has a reason (or two or ten) for being in this fight, and I love to hear the passion in their voices.

That's why it's so important to me that we celebrate the people who make all of this possible.

I've heard that the spaces on the Grassroots Donor Wall are filling up fast, and I'm deeply grateful for every single person who has chosen to be part of it.

**Every name on our Grassroots Donor Wall is a sign that our movement is growing. Every name up on our wall is one more person, one more family, who 's said that this fight is their fight, too.**

**[Will you contribute what makes sense for you today to own a place on our Grassroots Donor Wall-- and a piece of this movement?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852957c/45c30650/15771401e/66825e9d/4271938326/VEsHAw/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWdyYXNzcm9vdHMtZG9ub3Itd2FsbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiRU1JMTdGX0dEV19HRFdfRV8yMDE5MDgxMV9WMVMzX1AifQ==/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-grassroots-donor-wall?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMI17F_GDW_GDW_E_20190811_V1S3_P")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

subject: Iowa reminds me of home
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|

Being back in Iowa for the state fair was an absolute joy folks.

Iowa actually reminds me a lot of Ohio -- the scenery is beautiful, the people are welcoming, and the communities are filled with hard-working families just trying to get by -- often working multiple jobs with no real help from Washington. **This needs to change. And I 'm the one to change it.**
****[CONTRIBUTE $3 TO CREATE LASTING CHANGE-- TOGETHER](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+CONTRIBUTE+ask+TO+CREATE+LASTING+CHANGE+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_1_1&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_1_1")**** Based on my time talking to Iowans, it's clear that we share the same priorities: protecting access to health care, investing in our communities, addressing the opioid crisis, getting people back to work, supporting our unions -- the list goes on and on. I've been sharing our innovative plan to not only bring green jobs to places like Iowa but to invest in our workforce and compete with China in the renewable energy sector.

Winning Iowa is critical to winning the nomination and taking on Donald Trump in 2020 -- but I'm more concerned about winning over the people of Iowa and listening to their concerns and grievances.

name: Jim Hood HQ
subject: A casino for special interests
body:
|
---
For too long, our legislative leaders have run our state like a casino for special interests -- collecting chips, handing out favors -- and Mississippians deserve better.

Friend,

For too long, our legislative leaders have run our state like a casino for special interests -- collecting chips, handing out favors -- and Mississippians deserve better. Jim has spent his entire career as district attorney and attorney general going after illegal corruption. As governor, Jim will clean up our state government by going after _legal_ corruption and ensuring our state government is putting the interests of hardworking Mississippians first. **Will you sign our petition to show your support for Jim 's lobbying reforms?**
[**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://act.myngp.com/el/VGQvJa0j1EA_gS8JnqJYRdLTj78gGCG7Es6e3Db8B3o=/xsS8baxIUggflUlkYIf3farccdSuT3q_k2GpyLHkRyo=)
---
Jim Hood's lobbying reforms will go after every instance of taxpayers getting ripped off, every special insider deal, and every wasteful giveaway. That means:
* Subjecting our legislature to the Public Records Act, which would require the disclosure of any and all communication between lobbyists and legislators
* Banning campaign contributions while the legislature is in session
* Banning gifts of any kind from lobbyists, like:
\- Fancy drinks and meals
\- Tickets to concerts
\- Tickets to sports games

Jim is running for governor to put a stop to shady, backroom deals. These reforms are the change we need to ensure that our government is working for us, not special interest groups.

**Sign our petition to support Jim 's proposals to make Mississippi's state government open and honest.**
[**SIGN HERE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/VGQvJa0j1EA_gS8JnqJYRdLTj78gGCG7Es6e3Db8B3o=/xsS8baxIUggflUlkYIf3farccdSuT3q_k2GpyLHkRyo=)

subject: did you see Tim's note?

body:
|
---
Hey Team,

I just wanted to bump Tim's message. Our trip to Iowa was an incredible experience.

Watching Tim speak to families and communities about green jobs and the economy has upped my confidence in this campaign -- he truly knows how to talk to people and create an actionable plan to meet their needs -- and trust me, that is rare.

Thanks for being on this tour with us! [**https://secure.timryanforamerica.com/travel-fund**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+httpssecuretimryanforamericacomtravelfun&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2")

**Begin Forwarded Message:**

> Being back in Iowa for the state fair was an absolute joy folks.
>
> Iowa actually reminds me a lot of Ohio -- the scenery is beautiful, the people are welcoming, and the communities are filled with hard working families just trying to get by -- often working multiple jobs with no real help from Washington. **This needs to change. And I 'm the one to change it.**
> ****[CONTRIBUTE $3 TO CREATE LASTING CHANGE-- TOGETHER](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+CONTRIBUTE+ask+TO+CREATE+LASTING+CHANGE+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2")**** Based on my time talking to Iowans, it's clear that we share the same priorities: protecting access to health care, investing in our communities, addressing the opioid crisis, getting people back to work, supporting our unions -- the list goes on and on. I've been sharing our innovative plan to not only bring green jobs to places like Iowa but to invest in our workforce and compete with China in the renewable energy sector.
>
> Winning Iowa is critical to winning the nomination and taking on Donald Trump in 2020 -- but I'm more concerned about winning over the people of Iowa and listening to their concerns and grievances.
>
> [**Can you help me make sure the team and I have what it takes to tour early states like Iowa and talk to folks about the important issues?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+Can+you+help+me+make+sure+the+team+and+I&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2")
> **[](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2")**
> [**https://secure.timryanforamerica.com/travel-fund**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=5+-+httpssecuretimryanforamericacomtravelfun&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190811_2_2")
>
> Appreciate it!
> Tim

subject: We're falling behind

body:
| | |

| Team, Jenna, our deputy campaign manager, has kept me updated on our grassroots fundraising. She told me that yesterday we fell $10,227 short of our goal. If we want to reach $350,000 by August 15th, we need to raise $160,000 by midnight tonight. **Team, will you chip in to help us raise $160,000 by midnight to keep us on track? Here 's a link to contribute: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190811-em3](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190811-em3?refcode=em_fr_20190811_CB_20190811-fr-CB-jrlbump-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Thanks for helping us get there,

The texts from Cory about our grassroots fundraising keep coming -- he's been really excited about how it's been going since the debate. **I _really_ want to be able to tell him that we can reach $160,000 this weekend.** **[](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190811-em3?refcode=em_fr_20190811_CB_20190811-fr-CB-jrlbump-actives_actives_i1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** But if I'm being honest, we're falling behind our projections for the week and need to catch up this weekend. We raised our mid-month goal to $350,000 because with nine candidates on the fall debate stage, it's essential we have the resources to help Cory stand out -- and that means meeting every goal we set. **[So team, will you chip in right now to help us reach $160,000 this weekend? There are only a few hours left until midnight, and when I wake up tomorrow, I really want to be able to tell Cory we're on track to hitting our goal.](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190811-em3?refcode=em_fr_20190811_CB_20190811-fr-CB-jrlbump-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Let's show the boss what this team can do,
Jenna Jenna Lowenstein
Deputy Campaign Manager
Cory 2020 [](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190811-em3?refcode=em_fr_20190811_CB_20190811-fr-CB-jrlbump-actives_actives_contribbutton2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

body:
